app.name: Simple Router
app.tagline: Unified API Gateway for AI Coding Agents and OpenClaw
app.description: A single endpoint to access GPT, Qwen and many other models.

hero.title: Simple Router
hero.subtitle: Unified API Gateway for <highlight>AI Coding Agents</highlight> and <highlight>OpenClaw</highlight>.

packages.title: Service Plans
packages.subtitle: Flexible plans suitable for all needs: coding, n8n, chatbot

c.name: Plan C
features: About 100 requests/5 hours, with weekly and quota limits
Suitable for various coding agent tasks, OpenClaw

q.name: Plan Q
features: Only suitable for programming tools, coding agents

unified.title: Unified Access
unified.description: A single endpoint for Claude, Gemini, GPT and more. No need to manage multiple API keys.

compatible.title: OpenAI Compatible
compatible.description: Fully compatible with OpenAI API. Works with any tool that supports OpenAI.

performance.title: Optimized Performance
performance.description: Low latency, smart caching. Optimized for AI coding agents.

security.title: Safe & Secure
security.description: Your data is protected. Conversation content is not stored.

cta.title: Ready to Get Started?
cta.subtitle: Register via Telegram to get a free API key and start using it today.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.description: Simple Router is a unified API gateway for AI coding agents. Connect Claude Code, OpenCode and many other tools with the latest models: <opus>Claude Opus 4.6</opus>, <gemini>Gemini 3 Pro</gemini>.

claudeCode.title: Claude Code Integration
claudeCode.description: Comprehensive guide to setting up and optimizing Anthropic's Claude Code CLI with Simple Router.

step1.title: Introduction
step1.description: Claude Code is a revolutionary CLI tool that allows Claude to interact directly with your codebase, execute shell commands, and solve complex problems right in your terminal.

step2.title: Quick Install
step2.description: Install Claude Code globally via your preferred package manager.

step3.title: Configuration
step3.description: Create a settings.json file at the following path:
step3.warning: ⚠️ Important: You need to replace the Model ID in the configuration with one of the models from the <link>model list</link> for the system to work.

step4.title: Launch
step4.description: After setup is complete, launch Claude Code:

step5.title: Security Confirmation
step5.description: When running the claude command for the first time, you will see a security confirmation screen. Press 1 to select "Yes, proceed" and continue.
step5.note: Claude Code needs permission to read, write, and execute files in the current directory. Only use with projects from trusted sources.

step6.title: Complete
step6.description: When you see the screen below, you have successfully set up! You can now start using Claude Code.

tip.text: Tip: Replace {apiKey} with your actual API key. You can get the key from @simple_route_bot on Telegram.

opencode.title: OpenCode Setup
opencode.description: Configure OpenCode Agent to work seamlessly with Simple Router's unified API gateway.

step1.title: Introduction
step1.description: OpenCode is a powerful open source AI agent that runs in your terminal. By connecting it with Simple Router, you can orchestrate multiple models through a single configuration.

step2.title: Installation
step2.description: Install OpenCode CLI. See more <link>official download page</link>.

step3.title: Configuration
step3.description: Create a configuration file to point OpenCode to your Simple Router. See more <link>model configuration guide</link>.
step3.note: After configuration, run /models in OpenCode to select a model.
step3.warning: ⚠️ Important: You need to replace the Model ID in the configuration with one of the models from the <link>model list</link> for the system to work.

kilo.title: Kilo Code Setup
kilo.description: Configure Kilo Code VS Code extension to work with Simple Router.

step1.title: Introduction
step1.description: Kilo Code is a powerful VS Code extension that brings autonomous AI agents into your editor. Configure it to route requests through Simple Router for unified management.

step2.title: Installation
step2.description: Install Kilo Code from the VS Code marketplace or Open VSX registry.

step3.title: Configuration
step3.description: Open VS Code settings and configure Kilo Code to use Simple Router:
step3.note: Replace model IDs with actual models from the model list.

rooCode.title: Roo Code Setup
rooCode.description: Configure Roo Code autonomous agent for VS Code with Simple Router.

step1.title: Introduction
step1.description: Roo Code is a fully autonomous AI agent that works in your editor. By connecting it with Simple Router, you get centralized control over models and costs.

step2.title: Installation
step2.description: Install Roo Code from the VS Code marketplace.

step3.title: Configuration
step3.description: Configure Roo Code to use Simple Router as your API provider.
step3.note: Make sure to select the appropriate model for your tasks.

cline.title: Cline Setup
cline.description: Configure Cline open source coding agent with Simple Router.

step1.title: Introduction
step1.description: Cline is an open source AI assistant capable of handling complex software development tasks. Connect it to Simple Router for efficient model orchestration.

step2.title: Installation
step2.description: Install Cline from the VS Code marketplace.

step3.title: Configuration
step3.description: Configure Cline to use Simple Router as your API endpoint.
step3.note: Use the appropriate model ID for your coding tasks.

amp.description: Configure Amp agent with Simple Router.

openclaw.title: OpenClaw Setup
openclaw.description: Configure OpenClaw AI agent to work with Simple Router's unified API gateway.

step1.title: Introduction
step1.description: OpenClaw is a powerful open-source AI assistant that runs locally on your machine. It can connect to various messaging platforms and provides an always-available AI assistant with advanced capabilities.

step2.title: Installation
step2.description: Install OpenClaw using the official installer script. Requires Node.js 22+.
step2.note: On Windows, WSL2 is strongly recommended.

step3.title: Configuration
step3.description: Configure OpenClaw to use Simple Router as your model provider. You can use CLI commands or edit the config file directly.
step3.note: Replace YOUR_API_KEY with your actual API key from Telegram @simple_route_bot. Model IDs: c-gpt-5.4, c-gpt-5.3-codex, q-qwen3-coder-plus, etc.

step4.title: Run
step4.description: Start the OpenClaw gateway and connect to Simple Router:
step4.note: Make sure Simple Router is running before starting OpenClaw.

step5.title: Messaging Channels
step5.description: OpenClaw supports many messaging platforms. Configure your preferred channel:
